summary: High‑energy second set with soaring Bird Song & epic Morning Dew
blurb: Second set erupts with a blistering Playing In The Band → Crazy Fingers → Uncle John's Band sequence and a monumental Morning Dew. Jerry dances and solos fiercely while Phil’s bass roars on Cold Rain and Brent’s keyboards blaze throughout. Highlights include Bird Song, Althea and a guest‑sax‑flavored Iko Iko.
review: The December 27, 1989 show at the Oakland Coliseum opened with a solid first set – Cold Rain And Snow, Greatest Story Ever Told and a tight Althea – but it was the second set that truly lit the night. A fiery Playing In The Band segued into Crazy Fingers and Uncle John's Band before launching into an expansive Drum‑Space‑Wheel excursion, all building toward a thunderous Morning Dew that had the entire house on its feet. Jerry Garcia was in full show‑man mode, dancing in front of his mic and delivering scorching solos on both Bird Song and the climactic Dew, even when a brief mix dropout tried to steal the spotlight. Phil Lesh’s new Ken Smith bass sang with “blasting” intensity, especially on Cold Rain And Snow, while Brent Mydland’s piano and synth work added psychedelic color throughout, pushing the jam into higher gear. A surprise guest saxophone on Iko Iko gave the set an extra burst of excitement, and the encore of Johnny B. Goode and Black Muddy River rounded out a night that felt both gritty and fun. Across multiple audience and matrix recordings the performance shines as one of the most energetic New Year’s‑run shows of ’89, making it a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All nine recorded analyses are marked positive, repeatedly praising the band’s high‑energy playing, standout jams (Bird Song, Morning Dew) and guest sax on Iko Iko. No negative sentiment appears, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear, powerful mix that captures the band’s energy; only minor dropouts during Morning Dew, making it the most complete and vivid listening experience.

key_highlights: Explosive second‑set jam culminating in a legendary Morning Dew.
Guest saxophone on Iko Iko adds unique flavor to the set.
Althea shines as an opening‑night highlight.

band_performance.Jerry: Dances in front of the mic, delivering scorching solos on Bird Song and Morning Dew; occasional mix dropout noted but his infectious joy drives the show.
band_performance.Phil: Hot and "blasting" throughout, especially on Cold Rain where his new Ken Smith bass tones shine with powerful bass‑bombs.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is on fire – piano fills add psychedelic color, pushes the Dew higher, and provides strong support through the late‑show jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums (with Bill) are described as "firing on all cylinders," keeping the high‑energy momentum steady.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey, the rhythm section drives the set forward; occasional mix issues momentarily lose Jerry but otherwise solid.
